coreConcept: The Water Cycle is like Earth's big recycling system for water! Water is always moving all around our planet, changing from liquid to gas and back again. This amazing journey helps make sure we always have fresh water to drink and for plants to grow.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The Sun's warmth gives water the energy to change into an invisible gas called water vapor and float up into the sky.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Oceans, Lakes, and Rivers
keyActorsAndComponents.description: These are the big pools where most of Earth's water lives, ready to start its journey into the air.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Clouds are like fluffy sky-bags made of tiny water droplets or ice crystals, waiting to release rain or snow.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Plants
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Plants drink water from the ground and then breathe some of it out as vapor into the air, a process called transpiration.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Mountains and Land
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Mountains and land shape where water flows after it falls, directing it into rivers and back to the oceans or underground.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation
storyOfProcess.description: The Sun heats up water in oceans, lakes, and rivers, turning it into an invisible gas called water vapor that rises into the sky.

storyOfProcess.name: Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: High up in the sky, the water vapor gets cold and turns back into tiny liquid droplets or ice crystals, clumping together to form clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When clouds get too full of water droplets, they release it back to Earth as rain, snow, sleet, or hail.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection/Runoff
storyOfProcess.description: The water that falls collects in oceans, lakes, rivers, or soaks into the ground, ready to start its journey all over again.

corePrinciples.name: Water is always moving
corePrinciples.description: Water on Earth is never still; it's constantly traveling through the sky, land, and oceans in a continuous loop.

corePrinciples.name: Water changes forms
corePrinciples.description: Water can be a liquid (like in a river), a gas (like vapor in the air), or a solid (like ice or snow), and it changes between these forms during its cycle.

corePrinciples.name: Essential for life
corePrinciples.description: The water cycle provides fresh water for all living things, making sure plants, animals, and humans have what they need to survive.
